export const DEFAULT_PAGES = [{
  id: 1,
  type: "cover",
  title: "A Word of Thanks to the Nation’s by the School Principal",
  subtitle: "A Story for Little Explorers",
  bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #deddda, #ffffff)",
  textColor: "#000000",
  fontSize: 26,
  image: "Images/ThankWordOne.png",
  style: {
    fontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
    titleAlign: "center",
    textAlign: "center",
    imageW: "100%",
    imageH: "80%",
    imageFit: "contains",
    imagePosition: "center",
    imageShadow: "0 8px 28px rgba(0,0,0,0.25)",
    imageRadius: 5,
    imageBorder: "4px solid #3c3c3c",
    gap: 10,
    padding: [24, 22],
    overlayCircles: true,
    contentOrder: ["title", "image"]
  }
}, {
  id: 2,
  type: "content",
  title: "A Word of Thanks to the Nation’s by the Academic Advisor",
  text: "Deep inside a sparkling forest, a tiny rabbit named Luna discovered something truly wonderful — a glowing golden acorn that hummed like a song.",
  bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #deddda, #ffffff)",
  textColor: "#000000",
  fontSize: 26,
  image: "Images/ThankWordTwo.png",
  style: {
    fontFamily: "'Nunito', sans-serif",
    titleFontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
    titleAlign: "center",
    textAlign: "center",
    imageW: "100%",
    imageH: "80%",
    imageFit: "contains",
    imagePosition: "center",
    imageRadius: 10,
    imageBorder: "4px solid #3c3c3c",
    imageShadow: "0 4px 16px rgba(46,125,50,0.3)",
    gap: 12,
    padding: [22, 20],
    cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.40)",
    cardRadius: 20,
    cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(165,214,167,0.6)",
    overlayCircles: true,
    contentOrder: ["title", "image"]
  }
}, {
  id: 3,
  type: "content",
  title: "The Wise Old Owl",
  text: "Luna ran to find her friend Oliver the Owl, who lived in the tallest oak tree. 'What does it mean?' she asked, holding up the shiny acorn.",
  bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #e3f2fd, #bbdefb)",
  textColor: "#1565c0",
  fontSize: 18,
  image: "Images/ThankWordThree.png",
  titleSize: 26,
  style: {
    fontFamily: "'Quicksand', sans-serif",
    titleFontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
    titleAlign: "left",
    textAlign: "left",
    imageW: 120,
    imageH: 120,
    imageFit: "contain",
    imagePosition: "center",
    imageRadius: 12,
    imageBorder: "3px dashed #90caf9",
    imageShadow: "0 6px 20px rgba(21,101,192,0.25)",
    gap: 10,
    padding: [20, 18],
    cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.35)",
    cardRadius: 14,
    cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(144,202,249,0.5)",
    factsBg: "rgba(227,242,253,0.55)",
    factsRadius: 12,
    factsBorder: "1px solid rgba(144,202,249,0.4)",
    overlayCircles: false,
    contentOrder: ["title", "image", "text", "facts"]
  },
  reference: {
    title: "Owls",
    facts: ["There are over 200 species of owls on every continent except Antarctica.", "Owls can rotate their heads up to 270° — but NOT all the way around.", "A group of owls is called a parliament.", "Oak trees can live for over 1,000 years and produce 20,000 acorns per year."],
    learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Owl"
  }
}, {
  id: 4,
  type: "content",
  title: "The Map Appears!",
  text: "Oliver smiled wisely. 'That acorn is a key!' Suddenly a magical map unfolded from inside the acorn — showing a path to the Rainbow Waterfall.",
  bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #fce4ec, #f8bbd0)",
  textColor: "#880e4f",
  fontSize: 18,
  image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/1436/1436820.png",
  titleSize: 26,
  style: {
    fontFamily: "'Courgette', cursive",
    titleFontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
    titleAlign: "center",
    textAlign: "center",
    imageW: 155,
    imageH: 130,
    // non-square — wider than tall
    imageFit: "contain",
    imagePosition: "center",
    imageRadius: 8,
    imageBorder: "4px solid #f48fb1",
    imageShadow: "0 6px 24px rgba(136,14,79,0.22)",
    gap: 12,
    padding: [22, 20],
    cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.32)",
    cardRadius: 22,
    cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(244,143,177,0.5)",
    factsBg: "rgba(252,228,236,0.55)",
    factsRadius: 16,
    overlayCircles: true,
    contentOrder: ["title", "image", "text", "facts"]
  },
  reference: {
    title: "Maps & Cartography",
    facts: ["The oldest known map is the Babylonian Map of the World, carved on clay around 600 BCE.", "Cartography is the science and art of making maps.", "Before GPS, explorers used stars, compasses, and hand-drawn charts to navigate.", "Treasure maps with 'X marks the spot' are mostly a Hollywood invention."]
    // learnMore omitted — no link on this page
  }
}, {
  id: 5,
  type: "content",
  title: "The Journey Begins",
  text: "Luna and Oliver packed a tiny backpack with berries and honey. Together they hopped and flew through the shimmering trees, following the golden path.",
  bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #fff8e1, #ffecb3)",
  textColor: "#e65100",
  fontSize: 18,
  image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/3106/3106776.png",
  titleSize: 26,
  style: {
    fontFamily: "'Pacifico', cursive",
    titleFontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
    textFontFamily: "'Nunito', sans-serif",
    titleAlign: "center",
    textAlign: "center",
    imageW: 145,
    imageH: 145,
    imageFit: "contain",
    imagePosition: "center",
    imageShadow: "0 8px 20px rgba(230,81,0,0.20)",
    gap: 12,
    padding: [22, 20],
    cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.42)",
    cardRadius: 24,
    cardBorder: "2px solid rgba(255,224,130,0.7)",
    factsBg: "rgba(255,248,225,0.60)",
    factsRadius: 14,
    factsBorder: "1px solid rgba(255,202,40,0.5)",
    overlayCircles: true,
    contentOrder: ["image", "title", "text", "facts"]
  },
  reference: {
    title: "Forest Berries & Honey",
    facts: ["Wild berries like blueberries and raspberries grow in temperate forests.", "A single bee produces only 1/12 teaspoon of honey in its lifetime.", "Bears, birds, and foxes rely on berries as a major food source.", "Foraging (finding food in the wild) is one of humanity's oldest survival skills."],
    learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foraging"
  }
}, {
  id: 6,
  type: "content",
  title: "Rainbow Waterfall",
  text: "At last they found it! The Rainbow Waterfall sang seven colors into the sky. Luna placed the acorn at its base — and flowers bloomed everywhere! 🌸🌼🌺",
  bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #ede7f6, #d1c4e9)",
  textColor: "#4527a0",
  fontSize: 18,
  image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/1931/1931792.png",
  titleSize: 26,
  style: {
    fontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
    titleAlign: "center",
    textAlign: "center",
    imageW: 160,
    imageH: 160,
    imageFit: "cover",
    imagePosition: "center",
    imageRadius: 50,
    imageBorder: "5px solid #ce93d8",
    imageShadow: "0 0 30px rgba(206,147,216,0.6), 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.15)",
    gap: 12,
    padding: [22, 20],
    cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.30)",
    cardRadius: 20,
    cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(206,147,216,0.5)",
    factsBg: "rgba(237,231,246,0.55)",
    factsRadius: 16,
    factsBorder: "1px solid rgba(186,104,200,0.3)",
    overlayCircles: true,
    contentOrder: ["image", "title", "text", "facts"]
  },
  reference: {
    title: "Rainbows & Waterfalls",
    facts: ["Rainbows form when sunlight refracts inside water droplets.", "The seven colors: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, violet.", "Angel Falls in Venezuela is the world's tallest waterfall at 979 m.", "Waterfalls near mist can produce 'moonbows' lit by moonlight at night."],
    learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rainbow"
  }
}, {
  id: 7,
  type: "end",
  title: "The End",
  subtitle: "You are a wonderful reader! ⭐",
  bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #1a237e, #0d47a1)",
  textColor: "#fff",
  fontSize: 32,
  image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/2961/2961935.png",
  emoji: "💫",
  style: {
    fontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
    titleAlign: "center",
    textAlign: "center",
    imageW: 180,
    imageH: 180,
    imageFit: "contain",
    imagePosition: "center",
    imageRadius: 50,
    imageBorder: "4px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.4)",
    imageShadow: "0 0 40px rgba(255,255,255,0.25), 0 6px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)",
    gap: 16,
    padding: [28, 22],
    cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.12)",
    cardRadius: 20,
    cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.2)",
    factsBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.10)",
    factsRadius: 14,
    factsBorder: "1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.2)",
    overlayCircles: true,
    contentOrder: ["image", "title", "subtitle", "facts", "emoji"]
  },
  reference: {
    title: "The Moon & Stars",
    facts: ["The Moon is about 384,400 km from Earth and orbits every ~27 days.", "There are an estimated 200–400 billion stars in our Milky Way galaxy.", "The Moon's gravity is responsible for Earth's ocean tides.", "Reading stories before bed helps children develop vocabulary and imagination."],
    learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moon"
  }
}];

sourcesContent:
export const DEFAULT_PAGES = [
  {
    id: 1,
    type: "cover",
    title: "A Word of Thanks to the Nation’s by the School Principal",
    subtitle: "A Story for Little Explorers",
    bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #deddda, #ffffff)",
    textColor: "#000000",
    fontSize: 26,
    image: "Images/ThankWordOne.png",
    style: {
      fontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
      titleAlign: "center",
      textAlign: "center",
      imageW: "100%",
      imageH: "80%",
      imageFit: "contains",
      imagePosition: "center",
      imageShadow: "0 8px 28px rgba(0,0,0,0.25)",
      imageRadius: 5,
      imageBorder: "4px solid #3c3c3c",
      gap: 10,
      padding: [24, 22],
      overlayCircles: true,
      contentOrder: ["title","image" ],
    },
   
  },
 
  {
    id: 2,
    type: "content",
    title: "A Word of Thanks to the Nation’s by the Academic Advisor",
    text: "Deep inside a sparkling forest, a tiny rabbit named Luna discovered something truly wonderful — a glowing golden acorn that hummed like a song.",
    bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #deddda, #ffffff)",
    textColor: "#000000",
    fontSize: 26,
    image: "Images/ThankWordTwo.png" , 
    style: {
      fontFamily: "'Nunito', sans-serif",
      titleFontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
      titleAlign: "center",
      textAlign: "center", 
      imageW: "100%",
      imageH: "80%",
      imageFit: "contains",
      imagePosition: "center",
      imageRadius: 10,
      imageBorder: "4px solid #3c3c3c",
      imageShadow: "0 4px 16px rgba(46,125,50,0.3)",
      gap: 12,
      padding: [22, 20],
      cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.40)",
      cardRadius: 20,
      cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(165,214,167,0.6)",
      overlayCircles: true,
      contentOrder: [ "title", "image"],
    },
  },
 
  {
    id: 3,
    type: "content",
    title: "The Wise Old Owl",
    text: "Luna ran to find her friend Oliver the Owl, who lived in the tallest oak tree. 'What does it mean?' she asked, holding up the shiny acorn.",
    bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #e3f2fd, #bbdefb)",
    textColor: "#1565c0",
    fontSize: 18,
    image: "Images/ThankWordThree.png",
    titleSize: 26,
    style: {
      fontFamily: "'Quicksand', sans-serif",
      titleFontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
      titleAlign: "left",
      textAlign: "left",
      imageW: 120,
      imageH: 120,
      imageFit: "contain",
      imagePosition: "center",
      imageRadius: 12,
      imageBorder: "3px dashed #90caf9",
      imageShadow: "0 6px 20px rgba(21,101,192,0.25)",
      gap: 10,
      padding: [20, 18],
      cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.35)",
      cardRadius: 14,
      cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(144,202,249,0.5)",
      factsBg: "rgba(227,242,253,0.55)",
      factsRadius: 12,
      factsBorder: "1px solid rgba(144,202,249,0.4)",
      overlayCircles: false,
      contentOrder: ["title", "image", "text", "facts"],
    },
    reference: {
      title: "Owls",
      facts: [
        "There are over 200 species of owls on every continent except Antarctica.",
        "Owls can rotate their heads up to 270° — but NOT all the way around.",
        "A group of owls is called a parliament.",
        "Oak trees can live for over 1,000 years and produce 20,000 acorns per year.",
      ],
      learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Owl",
    },
  },
 
  {
    id: 4,
    type: "content",
    title: "The Map Appears!",
    text: "Oliver smiled wisely. 'That acorn is a key!' Suddenly a magical map unfolded from inside the acorn — showing a path to the Rainbow Waterfall.",
    bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #fce4ec, #f8bbd0)",
    textColor: "#880e4f",
    fontSize: 18,
    image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/1436/1436820.png",
    titleSize: 26,
    style: {
      fontFamily: "'Courgette', cursive",
      titleFontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
      titleAlign: "center",
      textAlign: "center",
      imageW: 155,
      imageH: 130,               // non-square — wider than tall
      imageFit: "contain",
      imagePosition: "center",
      imageRadius: 8,
      imageBorder: "4px solid #f48fb1",
      imageShadow: "0 6px 24px rgba(136,14,79,0.22)",
      gap: 12,
      padding: [22, 20],
      cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.32)",
      cardRadius: 22,
      cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(244,143,177,0.5)",
      factsBg: "rgba(252,228,236,0.55)",
      factsRadius: 16,
      overlayCircles: true,
      contentOrder: ["title", "image", "text", "facts"],
    },
    reference: {
      title: "Maps & Cartography",
      facts: [
        "The oldest known map is the Babylonian Map of the World, carved on clay around 600 BCE.",
        "Cartography is the science and art of making maps.",
        "Before GPS, explorers used stars, compasses, and hand-drawn charts to navigate.",
        "Treasure maps with 'X marks the spot' are mostly a Hollywood invention.",
      ],
      // learnMore omitted — no link on this page
    },
  },
 
  {
    id: 5,
    type: "content",
    title: "The Journey Begins",
    text: "Luna and Oliver packed a tiny backpack with berries and honey. Together they hopped and flew through the shimmering trees, following the golden path.",
    bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #fff8e1, #ffecb3)",
    textColor: "#e65100",
    fontSize: 18,
    image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/3106/3106776.png",
    titleSize: 26,
    style: {
      fontFamily: "'Pacifico', cursive",
      titleFontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
      textFontFamily: "'Nunito', sans-serif",
      titleAlign: "center",
      textAlign: "center",
      imageW: 145,
      imageH: 145,
      imageFit: "contain",
      imagePosition: "center",
      imageShadow: "0 8px 20px rgba(230,81,0,0.20)",
      gap: 12,
      padding: [22, 20],
      cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.42)",
      cardRadius: 24,
      cardBorder: "2px solid rgba(255,224,130,0.7)",
      factsBg: "rgba(255,248,225,0.60)",
      factsRadius: 14,
      factsBorder: "1px solid rgba(255,202,40,0.5)",
      overlayCircles: true,
      contentOrder: ["image", "title", "text", "facts"],
    },
    reference: {
      title: "Forest Berries & Honey",
      facts: [
        "Wild berries like blueberries and raspberries grow in temperate forests.",
        "A single bee produces only 1/12 teaspoon of honey in its lifetime.",
        "Bears, birds, and foxes rely on berries as a major food source.",
        "Foraging (finding food in the wild) is one of humanity's oldest survival skills.",
      ],
      learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foraging",
    },
  },
 
  {
    id: 6,
    type: "content",
    title: "Rainbow Waterfall",
    text: "At last they found it! The Rainbow Waterfall sang seven colors into the sky. Luna placed the acorn at its base — and flowers bloomed everywhere! 🌸🌼🌺",
    bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #ede7f6, #d1c4e9)",
    textColor: "#4527a0",
    fontSize: 18,
    image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/1931/1931792.png",
    titleSize: 26,
    style: {
      fontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
      titleAlign: "center",
      textAlign: "center",
      imageW: 160,
      imageH: 160,
      imageFit: "cover",
      imagePosition: "center",
      imageRadius: 50,
      imageBorder: "5px solid #ce93d8",
      imageShadow: "0 0 30px rgba(206,147,216,0.6), 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.15)",
      gap: 12,
      padding: [22, 20],
      cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.30)",
      cardRadius: 20,
      cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(206,147,216,0.5)",
      factsBg: "rgba(237,231,246,0.55)",
      factsRadius: 16,
      factsBorder: "1px solid rgba(186,104,200,0.3)",
      overlayCircles: true,
      contentOrder: ["image", "title", "text", "facts"],
    },
    reference: {
      title: "Rainbows & Waterfalls",
      facts: [
        "Rainbows form when sunlight refracts inside water droplets.",
        "The seven colors: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, violet.",
        "Angel Falls in Venezuela is the world's tallest waterfall at 979 m.",
        "Waterfalls near mist can produce 'moonbows' lit by moonlight at night.",
      ],
      learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rainbow",
    },
  },
 
  {
    id: 7,
    type: "end",
    title: "The End",
    subtitle: "You are a wonderful reader! ⭐",
    bg: "linear-gradient(135deg, #1a237e, #0d47a1)",
    textColor: "#fff",
    fontSize: 32,
    image: "https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/2961/2961935.png",
    emoji: "💫",
    style: {
      fontFamily: "'Bubblegum Sans', cursive",
      titleAlign: "center",
      textAlign: "center",
      imageW: 180,
      imageH: 180,
      imageFit: "contain",
      imagePosition: "center",
      imageRadius: 50,
      imageBorder: "4px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.4)",
      imageShadow: "0 0 40px rgba(255,255,255,0.25), 0 6px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)",
      gap: 16,
      padding: [28, 22],
      cardBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.12)",
      cardRadius: 20,
      cardBorder: "1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.2)",
      factsBg: "rgba(255,255,255,0.10)",
      factsRadius: 14,
      factsBorder: "1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.2)",
      overlayCircles: true,
      contentOrder: ["image", "title", "subtitle", "facts", "emoji"],
    },
    reference: {
      title: "The Moon & Stars",
      facts: [
        "The Moon is about 384,400 km from Earth and orbits every ~27 days.",
        "There are an estimated 200–400 billion stars in our Milky Way galaxy.",
        "The Moon's gravity is responsible for Earth's ocean tides.",
        "Reading stories before bed helps children develop vocabulary and imagination.",
      ],
      learnMore: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moon",
    },
  },
];
